name: google-analytics
description: Run GA4 reports and realtime reports, and discover Analytics accounts and properties

tools.name: List Account Summaries
tools.description: List GA4 accounts and their properties accessible to the connected user (use this to find property IDs)

pageToken.description: Pagination token from a previous response

historicalSummary.omitted: property detail fields beyond names and IDs

tools.name: Run Report
tools.description: Run a GA4 report for a property over one or more date ranges

dimensions.description: Dimensions to break down by, e.g. [{"name":"country"}]

dimensionFilter.description: Optional GA4 FilterExpression to filter dimension values

tools.name: Run Realtime Report
tools.description: Run a GA4 realtime report showing activity from the last 30 minutes

dimensions.description: Dimensions to break down by, e.g. [{"name":"country"}]

tools.name: Get Report Metadata
tools.description: List the dimensions and metrics available for a GA4 property (use before building reports)

prompts.title: Traffic summary
prompts.prompt: Show active users and sessions for my Google Analytics property over the last 7 days, broken down by country.

prompts.title: Who's online now
prompts.prompt: Show the number of active users on my site right now using Google Analytics realtime data.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Google Analytics Setup

steps.title: Create or reuse a Google Cloud project
steps.description: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com and select or create the project that holds your OAuth credentials.

steps.title: Enable the Analytics APIs
steps.description: Enable both the Google Analytics Data API and the Google Analytics Admin API in the API Library.

steps.title: Configure OAuth credentials
steps.description: Under APIs & Services → Credentials, create (or reuse) an OAuth 2.0 Client ID of type Web application and add your callback URL (e.g. https://your-app.example.com/api/auth/google-analytics/callback) to the authorized redirect URIs.

steps.title: Set environment variables
steps.description: Add GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET to your .env. These are shared with the other Google connectors (Calendar, Gmail, Drive, Sheets).

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Connect your Google account, run List Account Summaries to find a property ID, then run a report with metrics [{"name":"activeUsers"}] and dateRanges [{"startDate":"7daysAgo","endDate":"today"}].

notes: This connector is read-only (analytics.readonly scope) and targets GA4 properties; Universal Analytics is sunset
Property IDs are numeric (e.g. 123456789) — not the G-XXXX measurement ID
